author: barnabasbusa
content: Now that we have the stable containers, how could we get rid of the 8192 deposit limit?

author: barnabasbusa

author: barnabasbusa
content: into your devnet 7 branch

author: barnabasbusa
content: https://github.com/sigp/lighthouse/pull/9337 this especially as lack of this creates forks

author: etan_status
content:
<@592004585506340885> may have a preferred way of raising the cap. 
it is no longer a change that breaks hashing, that's what 7688 gains.

author: jtraglia
content: I believe we have two options: (1) Define a new `MAX_DEPOSIT_REQUESTS_PER_PAYLOAD_GLOAS` set to a higher value (say 1 million) and use this value in the payload checks. (2) Simply remove `MAX_DEPOSIT_REQUESTS_PER_PAYLOAD` by marking it as deprecated in Gloas; then we remove the `assert len(requests.deposits) <= MAX_DEPOSIT_REQUESTS_PER_PAYLOAD` check in `apply_parent_execution_payload`.

author: jtraglia
content: Deposits are different than the other requests (withdrawals, consolidations, etc). In my opinion, it doesn't really make sense to have a defined maximum in the CL as we cannot queue them & it's already limited by the EL's gas limit.
